name: Complete an incomplete Su-Field
description: If a substance is not sufficiently controlled, complete the Su-Field model by introducing the missing element (substance or field).
applicability: System has an insufficient or incomplete interaction between components.

name: Add an internal additive to S1 or S2
description: Introduce an internal additive — a substance that modifies one of the existing substances to improve the interaction.
applicability: Direct modification of the existing substances is not possible or not sufficient.

name: Add an external additive
description: Introduce an external substance (from the environment or a cheap, readily available source) to improve the interaction.
applicability: Internal modification is prohibited or impractical.

name: Use the environment as an additive
description: Use resources already present in the environment (air, water, waste, etc.) as the missing or modifying element.
applicability: Adding new substances is restricted.

name: Introduce an additive temporarily
description: Introduce a temporary additive that performs its function and then disappears (dissolves, evaporates, decomposes).
applicability: Permanent additives are undesirable.

name: Use inflatable/foam structures
description: Introduce voids, bubbles, or foam as the modifying substance. Use inflatable structures that can be removed after use.
applicability: Need to fill a volume temporarily or create lightweight structures.

name: Add a field
description: If two substances exist but there is no effective interaction, introduce a field (mechanical, thermal, electromagnetic, etc.).
applicability: Substances are present but lack an effective interaction.

name: Use a field already present in the system
description: Instead of adding a new field, utilize a field that already exists in the system or its environment.
applicability: Adding a new field is undesirable or costly.

name: Eliminate harmful interaction by introducing S3
description: Introduce a third substance between S1 and S2 to block or absorb the harmful action.
applicability: Harmful interaction between two substances that cannot be separated.

name: Modify S1 or S2 to eliminate harm
description: Modify one of the existing substances so that the harmful interaction is eliminated while the useful one is preserved.
applicability: Adding a third substance is impractical.

name: Introduce a sacrificial substance
description: Introduce a cheap, disposable substance that absorbs the harmful action, protecting the main components.
applicability: The harmful action cannot be eliminated but can be redirected.

name: Introduce F2 to counteract harmful F1
description: Add a second field that counteracts or neutralizes the harmful field while preserving useful functions.
applicability: A harmful field exists and cannot be eliminated directly.

name: Switch off the harmful field via magnetic properties
description: Use ferromagnetic particles or electromagnets to control the harmful interaction — switch it off or redirect it.
applicability: The harmful interaction involves or can be influenced by magnetic fields.

name: Replace uncontrolled field with controllable one
description: Replace a mechanical, gravitational, or other poorly controllable field with an easily controllable one (electromagnetic, etc.).
applicability: The current field does not provide sufficient control or precision.

name: Increase field controllability by fragmentation
description: Increase controllability by fragmenting the substance (powder, granules, micro-particles) to improve interaction with the field.
applicability: The substance does not respond well to the applied field.

name: Transition to a chain Su-Field
description: If direct interaction is insufficient, build a chain: S1-F-S2-F2-S3, where an intermediate substance or field improves the result.
applicability: A single Su-Field model does not provide sufficient effect.

name: Transition to a double Su-Field
description: Add a second field acting on one of the substances to make the system more controllable or effective.
applicability: A single field does not provide enough control or effect.

name: Use matched rhythms
description: Match the frequency of the applied field to the natural frequency of the system (resonance) to amplify the effect.
applicability: The interaction effect needs to be amplified without increasing energy input.

name: Use ferromagnetic substance and magnetic field
description: Replace a non-magnetic substance with a ferromagnetic one and use magnetic field for control.
applicability: Need highly controllable, non-contact manipulation of substances.

name: Use magnetic liquid (ferrofluid)
description: Use ferrofluid (magnetic liquid) as a controllable substance that can change shape and position under magnetic field.
applicability: Need a controllable substance that can flow and fill complex geometries.

name: Use external ferromagnetic environment
description: If the substance cannot be made ferromagnetic, make the environment ferromagnetic and control via magnetic field.
applicability: The main substance cannot be replaced with a ferromagnetic one.

name: Use rheological fluids
description: Use electro- or magneto-rheological fluids that change viscosity under applied field.
applicability: Need controllable damping, stiffness, or flow resistance.

name: Transition to bi-system or poly-system
description: Combine two or more systems into a bi-system or poly-system to gain new emergent properties.
applicability: A single system has exhausted its evolutionary potential.

name: Develop links between system elements
description: Strengthen or create new links between elements of a bi-system or poly-system to improve cooperation.
applicability: Elements in a multi-system work independently when cooperation would be beneficial.

name: Increase differences between elements
description: Make the elements of a poly-system increasingly different from each other to handle a wider range of conditions.
applicability: A uniform poly-system does not cover all required conditions.

name: Collapse poly-system elements
description: After elements become sufficiently different, collapse them by giving each element the abilities of the others.
applicability: A complex poly-system needs simplification while retaining capabilities.

name: Assign opposite properties to whole and parts
description: Give the whole system one property and its parts the opposite (or vice versa).
applicability: The system must simultaneously exhibit contradictory properties.

name: Transition to micro-level
description: Replace the macro-level mechanism with a micro-level one (molecules, atoms, ions, fields) for better control and efficiency.
applicability: Macro-level mechanisms have reached their limit of precision or miniaturization.

name: Instead of detecting, change the system to eliminate the need
description: Redesign the system so that the measurement or detection is no longer needed.
applicability: The measurement itself is problematic; eliminating the need is simpler.

name: Use copies instead of originals for measurement
description: If direct measurement of the object is difficult, measure a copy, image, or model instead.
applicability: Direct contact measurement would damage or disturb the object.

name: Convert measurement to sequence of detections
description: Replace continuous measurement with detecting threshold crossings (go/no-go checks).
applicability: Continuous measurement is expensive or unreliable.

name: Measure using a Su-Field model
description: Build a Su-Field model for detection: add a substance or field whose change indicates the measured parameter.
applicability: Direct measurement is impossible; an indirect indicator is needed.

name: Use added detectable substance as marker
description: Add a substance (tracer, dye, radioactive marker) that makes the parameter visible or measurable.
applicability: The parameter of interest is not directly observable.

name: Use field changes for measurement
description: Apply a field and measure how the system modifies it (absorption, reflection, resonance change) to infer the parameter.
applicability: Adding substances is undesirable; non-contact measurement preferred.

name: Use ferromagnetic additives for detection
description: Add ferromagnetic particles and use magnetic field changes for detection or measurement.
applicability: Non-invasive detection is needed and magnetic methods are applicable.

name: Enhance measurement via resonance
description: Use resonance effects to amplify small signals that would otherwise be undetectable.
applicability: The signal to be measured is too weak for direct detection.

name: Enhance measurement via successive detection
description: Use a sequence of cumulative measurements or integrate over time to improve detection sensitivity.
applicability: Single-point measurement lacks sufficient signal-to-noise ratio.

name: Use bi-system or poly-system for measurement
description: Use two or more measuring systems in parallel and compare results for higher accuracy.
applicability: Single measurement system lacks accuracy or reliability.

name: Use differential measurement
description: Measure the difference between the target and a reference rather than the absolute value.
applicability: Absolute measurement is unreliable or affected by drift.

name: Measure change direction instead of magnitude
description: Instead of measuring the precise value, detect whether the parameter is increasing or decreasing.
applicability: Trend detection is sufficient and simpler than precise measurement.

name: Introduce substances under special conditions
description: Introduce substances that exist only under certain conditions (e.g., bubbles that collapse, ice that melts, foam that dissolves).
applicability: A substance is needed temporarily but must leave no trace.

name: Decompose a substance from the environment
description: Obtain the needed substance by decomposing something already present (electrolysis, chemical decomposition).
applicability: External substance supply is unavailable or expensive.

name: Introduce a field from the environment
description: Use naturally occurring fields (gravity, ambient temperature, sunlight, Earth's magnetism) instead of adding artificial ones.
applicability: Need a field without adding energy sources.

name: Use excess or waste resources
description: Use by-products, waste heat, excess vibration, or other system waste as useful resources.
applicability: System produces waste that could serve a function.

name: Derive substance from super/sub-system
description: Obtain the needed substance from the supersystem (environment) or by modifying a subsystem component.
applicability: Adding external substances is restricted.

name: Use the void as a substance
description: Use void, vacuum, empty space, porosity, or channels as a functional element.
applicability: A substance is needed but none should be added.

name: Use fields derived from existing substances
description: Use fields that substances already produce (thermal radiation, static charge, etc.) rather than adding new fields.
applicability: Need a field without additional field sources.

name: Use phase transitions for dual properties
description: Exploit phase transitions (solid↔liquid↔gas) to obtain substances with dual properties from a single material.
applicability: Need different material properties at different stages.

name: Use dual-state physical phenomena
description: Exploit physical effects that inherently produce two states (piezoelectric: pressure↔voltage, Peltier: hot side/cold side).
applicability: Need a controllable transformation between two physical states.

name: Use reversible chemical reactions
description: Use chemical reactions that can be reversed by changing conditions (temperature, pH, catalyst) to cycle between states.
applicability: Need to repeatedly switch between chemical states.

name: Self-service: make the system serve itself
description: Make the system perform auxiliary functions itself, eliminating the need for additional components or operators.
applicability: Additional components or manual operations are undesirable.

name: Use waste products of the system
description: Use waste energy, waste substances, or waste information produced by the system for useful secondary functions.
applicability: The system produces waste that currently serves no purpose.

name: Use structures with controllable properties
description: Replace rigid structures with ones whose properties (shape, density, permeability) can be dynamically controlled.
applicability: The system must adapt to changing conditions without redesign.

name: Dynamize: make the system or its parts movable
description: Make rigid elements flexible, stationary elements movable, and fixed structures adjustable.
applicability: The system is too rigid to adapt to varying requirements.

name: Create a self-regulating system
description: Use feedback to make the system self-regulating — it adjusts its own parameters to maintain optimal performance.
applicability: External control or operator intervention is undesirable.
